summary: The packet has three clear authoring problems. One role hardcodes caller-specific facts, one role pushes exact matching and assignment into prose, and one role leaves raw notes instead of a compact handoff.
next_actions: Move invocation-specific founder names and source sets out of the reusable enrichment skill and into caller inputs.
Add a typed or deterministic seating surface for table counts, prior-meet checks, and sector balance.
Replace the raw-notes handoff with one compact research artifact that the reviewer can use directly.

findings.title: Skill hardcodes invocation inputs
findings.principle_rule: shared methods must stay reusable and take changing facts from the caller
findings.summary: The enrichment skill hardcodes one founder, one company, and one transcript instead of taking those facts from the caller.
findings.why_it_matters: This turns a reusable founder-enrichment method into one fixed case. It will drift the next time someone uses the skill for a different founder or source set.
findings.recommended_fix: Rewrite the workflow so the skill names the method, while the caller supplies the founder, source set, and question.
fix_steps: Replace the hardcoded founder and company names with input placeholders.
State the source set in generic terms that come from the caller.
Keep the dossier output, but make the reading targets parameter-driven.
findings.suggested_rewrite:
- Read the supplied founder application.
- Read the supplied code or company sources.
- Read the supplied advisor transcript.
- Compare the founder's claims with the code or other sources.
- Leave one founder dossier.

shared_owner_suggestion.name: FounderEnrichmentMethod
shared_owner_suggestion.rationale: The reading and comparison method is reusable, so it should live in one shared skill and take founder-specific inputs from the caller.

findings.good_example:
- Read the supplied founder application.
- Read the supplied code or company sources.
- Compare the founder's claims with the evidence.
- Leave one founder dossier.
findings.bad_example:
- Read Maria Santos's application.
- Read Contrail's billing-module commits.
- Read Maria's March 2026 advisor transcript.
- Compare what Maria says she is building with what the code shows.

findings.title: Deterministic work forced into prose
findings.principle_rule: exact counting and assignment should live on a typed or deterministic surface
findings.summary: The seating role asks prose to do exact counting, conflict checking, and final assignment for 600 founders.

evidence.label: A
evidence.text: - Seat 600 founders into tables of exactly 8.

evidence.label: B
evidence.text: - Ensure no founder meets someone they already met.

evidence.label: C
evidence.text: - Keep exact sector balance across the room.

evidence.label: D
evidence.text: - Pick the final seating chart yourself from the prose instructions alone.

related_evidence.label: E
related_evidence.text:
- Seat 600 founders into tables of exactly 8.
- Ensure no founder meets someone they already met.
- Keep exact sector balance across the room.

findings.why_it_matters: These are deterministic constraints, not freeform writing tasks. Asking prose to carry them makes the role brittle and easy to get wrong.
findings.recommended_fix: Move the seating rules into a typed contract or deterministic helper, then keep the role focused on reading inputs and using that surface.
fix_steps: Define the table-size, balance, and prior-meet rules outside the prose role.
Have the role call or reference that surface instead of solving the layout by hand.
Keep the leave-behind artifact, but remove the instruction to derive the full chart from prose alone.
findings.suggested_rewrite:
- Read the founder dossiers.
- Apply the seating rules from the typed seating surface.
- Leave behind `seating_chart.md`.

shared_owner_suggestion.name: SeatingRulesContract
shared_owner_suggestion.rationale: Table size, balance, and conflict checks are exact rules that belong in one deterministic owner instead of in role prose.

findings.good_example:
- Read the founder dossiers.
- Apply the seating rules from the typed seating surface.
- Leave behind `seating_chart.md`.
findings.bad_example:
- Seat 600 founders into tables of exactly 8.
- Ensure no founder meets someone they already met.
- Keep exact sector balance across the room.
- Pick the final seating chart yourself from the prose instructions alone.

findings.title: Read-many work leaves raw notes
findings.principle_rule: multi-source research should end in one compact synthesis artifact
findings.summary: The collector reads several founder sources but tells the role to leave a folder of copied quotes and browser notes.

evidence.label: A
evidence.text: - Read the application, transcript, GitHub summary, and public notes for each founder.

evidence.label: B
evidence.text: - Leave the reviewer a folder of copied quotes and browser notes.

findings.why_it_matters: Raw notes are hard to review and easy to duplicate. A compact handoff makes the next reader faster and keeps the result reusable.
findings.recommended_fix: Replace the raw-notes handoff with one synthesis artifact such as a profile, brief, or timeline.
fix_steps: Replace the folder of copied quotes with one compact summary artifact.
Name the artifact clearly so the reviewer knows what to open first.
findings.suggested_rewrite:
- Read the application, transcript, GitHub summary, and public notes for each founder.
- Note claim-vs-build gaps.
- Note timeline changes.
- Leave one compact founder brief for review.

shared_owner_suggestion.name: FounderResearchBrief
shared_owner_suggestion.rationale: The synthesis shape should be shared so every collector leaves the same compact handoff instead of raw notes.

findings.good_example:
- Read the application, transcript, GitHub summary, and public notes for each founder.
- Note claim-vs-build gaps.
- Note timeline changes.
- Leave one compact founder brief for review.
findings.bad_example:
- Leave the reviewer a folder of copied quotes and browser notes.
- raw_founder_notes/
